programmes.show_description: ... And Amen: Poignant finale to the classic US comedy series, starring Alan Alda. In the closing days of the Korean War, the 4077 M*A*S*H Unit throw a party in the face of irrevocable changes. (1983)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.

... And Amen: Poignant finale to the classic US comedy series, starring Alan Alda. In the closing days of the Korean War, the 4077 M*A*S*H Unit throw a party in the face of irrevocable changes. (1983)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
All You Need is Love: Henry's objections towards Matthew's new girlfriend are intensified further when Enid makes a surprising discovery.
Suspect: Matthew discovers that the neighbour's son is not as perfect as everyone seems to believe. Will Henry be forced to apologise for doubting his son?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Part 1: Jonathan Smith, an angel, has been sent to help people on Earth. He befriends residents at Havencrest retirement home and the administrator Leslie Gordon.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Part 2: Jonathan is forced to reveal his real identity to Leslie's brother, Mark, in order to successfully complete his first assignment at Havencrest retirement home.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Way We Were: Henry makes plans to go to the club to escape what would have been his wedding anniversary. But will Matthew's plans to reunite his parents end happily ever after?
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Night Walker: A mysterious presence moving through the night strikes fear in the hearts of the Walton's Mountain residents.
The Cloudburst: In a desperate attempt to save his newspaper from bankruptcy, John-Boy sells his meadow to a mining company. But will the company's plans for the land devastate the area?
The Great Motorcycle Race: Jim-Bob takes his first steps into the world when he enters a race, riding Ike's motorcycle.
The Pony Cart: The oldest living Walton, Aunt Martha Corrine, travels to Walton's Mountain for an extended visit.
The Best Christmas: With the Walton children growing up, will this be the last time the entire family will be together at Christmas?
The Last Mustang: Grandpa is in a determined mood when a wild mustang is captured. Will he get his wish and see the mustang roaming free on Walton's Mountain?
The Rebellion: Grandma becomes upset when she is asked to share her role as church organist.
The Ferris Wheel: Elizabeth is found sleepwalking and having recurring nightmares about falling from a Ferris wheel. Meanwhile, Ben believes his height is affecting how people treat him.
The Elopement: Erin attempts to elope with Chad Marshall, and Jason takes over the store while Ike is away.
John's Crossroad: Due to the breakdown of the mill and mounting bills, John takes an office job in Charlottesville.
Keanu Reeves stars in this sci-fi thriller as a researcher trying to shield the discovery of a new power source from the US government. Morgan Freeman co-stars. (1996)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Keanu Reeves stars in this sci-fi thriller as a researcher trying to shield the discovery of a new power source from the US government. Morgan Freeman co-stars. (1996)
Freefalling action with Charlie Sheen, Nastassja Kinski and James Gandolfini. A maverick skydiving instructor becomes entangled in a murderous conspiracy involving Soviet spies and lost gold. (1994)
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Freefalling action with Charlie Sheen, Nastassja Kinski and James Gandolfini. A maverick skydiving instructor becomes entangled in a murderous conspiracy involving Soviet spies and lost gold. (1994)
White Gold: Thieves attempt to steal penicillin from the 4077th but Klinger catches one of them. Hawkeye and Trapper are suspicious when Colonel Flagg of the CIA arrives to investigate.
Abyssinia, Henry: Radar brings a message to Colonel Henry Blake; he's been discharged from the army. Everyone at the 4077th prepares to say goodbye to him as he heads for home.
Welcome to Korea - Part 1: Hawkeye returns from R&R to find Trapper has been discharged. He drives Radar at breakneck speed to the airport, but Trapper's plane has gone.
Welcome to Korea - Part 2: Hawkeye misses his chance to say goodbye to Trapper but picks up a replacement - good-natured BJ. On the road they see a woman injured in a minefield.
Dating Henry?: Matthew is intrigued to see who his father is trying to impress when Henry plans a romantic, candlelit dinner date at home.
Protest: Henry and Matthew's already strained relationship is tested further when Matthew becomes a vegetarian and animal rights activist.
Rumpole and the Married Lady: Rumpole accepts a divorce case involving his friend and colleague George Frobrisher. Meanwhile, his Chambers is embroiled in a petty theft case.
To Touch The Moon: Jonathan counsels a young runaway and a widow with a terminally ill son. Meanwhile, Mark deals with a thief abandoned by his folks.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
The Return Of The Masked Rider: Jonathan and Mark help ageing movie heroes confront the gang, Satan's Helpers. They must persuade a boxer not to throw a big fight when the gang bullies him.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
The Squeaky Wheel: The difficulties of people with a disability are addressed when an amputee joins a crusade following a false fire alarm.
Goodbye, Mr. Zelinka: Mr Zelinka is a beloved high-school teacher facing forced retirement because of his age. Jonathan and Mark suggest his students petition to keep him in school system.
To See the World: Taking responsibility for filling Johnny Johnson's head with exaggerated tales of adventure, Mr Edwards accompanies Johnny when he takes off to see the world.
Founder's Day: Founder's Day festivities promise fun and friendly competition for all except ageing logger Jim Tyler, who fears losing the log chopping contest to a younger Charles Ingalls.
The Richest Man in Walnut Grove: Charles and his employer Mr Hansen are anticipating a large income from a big job. But when go wrong, the family feel the financial strain.
The Career Girl: Erin graduates from high school with a lack of direction for the future. But will the opportunity to help John-Boy finish his novel help her to find a purpose?
The Hero: John-Boy decides to create a memorial in which Grandma Walton will be honoured.
Births, Deaths and Marriages: Brendan and Siobhan finally decide to tie the knot - but keeping it a secret could be a problem. Niamh and Ambrose, meanwhile, worry about their marriage.
Thick as Thieves: Linda and Len are surprised when they meet their first foster child. Elsewhere, the village comes together when the brewery hires a barmaid set on replacing Phyllis.
Dr Eleanor Bramwell has set her heart on becoming a leading surgeon. As a favour to her doctor father, eminent surgeon Sir Herbert Hamilton lets Eleanor assist at the hospital he runs.
An uplifting round-up of good news stories from the UK and around the world.
Rumpole and the Case of Identity: Can Rumpole successfully prove that his client is the victim of mistaken identity?
Mail Call... Again: Colonel Potter gets great news: he's going to become a grandpa. Frank gets bad news: he's going to be an ex-husband. His wife reveals that she knows all about Margaret.
The Price of Tomato Juice: Radar learns that Potter likes tomato juice and tries to procure some for him. Hawkeye and BJ warn him that a request will quickly result in a complicated ordeal.
Dear Ma: Radar writes to his mother with details of the foot inspection at the 4077th. Potter is shot in the backside but refuses anaesthetic so he can watch Hawkeye operate.
Der Tag: Hawkeye and BJ place a toe tag on a passed-out Frank as part of a drunken practical joke, but the joke backfires on them when he is accidentally shipped out to the front lines.
Hawkeye: Hawkeye is rescued by a Korean family when he is injured in a jeep accident. He knows how dangerous it can be to fall asleep with a concussion, so he talks to the family non-stop.
Some 38th Parallels: Frank becomes increasingly paranoid that the locals are stealing the camp's garbage, so he decides to auction off 150 pounds of it.
The Novocaine Mutiny: Frank charges Hawkeye with mutiny, but they each have different views of the circumstances leading to the court-martial.
